anthracnose.title: Anthracnose (Colletotrichum)
anthracnose.summary: Sunken black lesions with orange spore masses.
anthracnose.detailed_analysis: Latent fungal infection that develops as fruit sugar levels rise. Spots coalesce into large necrotic areas.
anthracnose.retail_spec: Class 1: Zero. Class 2: Max 2 small spots.

latex_stains.title: Latex Staining
latex_stains.summary: Black/brown resinous streaks on the peel.
latex_stains.detailed_analysis: Sap oxidation occurs when fruit is de-handed without proper washing. Purely aesthetic but reduces premium value.

thrips_scarring.title: Rust Thrips Damage
thrips_scarring.detailed_analysis: Feeding damage from Chaetanaphothrips. Skin becomes rough and may crack during ripening.

mechanical_bruising.title: Mechanical Impact Bruise
mechanical_bruising.summary: Flat or sunken dark areas on the peel.
mechanical_bruising.detailed_analysis: Cellular rupture due to physical impact. Leads to localized softening and accelerated rot.
mechanical_bruising.action: Sort and remove heavily bruised fingers.

sooty_mould.title: Sooty Mould
sooty_mould.summary: Black powdery film on skin or crown.
sooty_mould.detailed_analysis: Superficial fungus growing on insect honeydew. Can be wiped off but looks unappealing.
sooty_mould.action: Wipe with damp cloth or downgrade.

sunscald.title: Sunscald
sunscald.summary: Bleached or bronzed patches on upper fruit.
sunscald.detailed_analysis: Damage to chlorophyll from direct UV exposure. Tissue becomes hard and fails to ripen.

crown_rot.title: Crown Rot
crown_rot.summary: Soft decay at the finger attachment point.
crown_rot.detailed_analysis: Fungal complex spreading from the crown into the neck. Risks 'finger drop' and total pack failure.

chilling_injury.title: Chilling Injury (Smoky Peel)
chilling_injury.summary: Internal vascular browning and grey peel.
chilling_injury.detailed_analysis: Exposure below 13°C destroys cells. Causes smoky/dull appearance and 'hard-core' pulp.

ship_ripe.title: Ship Ripe / Green Ripe
ship_ripe.summary: Soft fruit that remains green in color.
ship_ripe.detailed_analysis: Physiological disorder where pulp softens while skin chlorophyll remains stable. High ethylene risk to other fruit.
ship_ripe.action: Remove immediately to save balance of load.

cigar_end_rot.title: Cigar-End Rot
cigar_end_rot.summary: Dry, ash-like rot at the flower end.
cigar_end_rot.detailed_analysis: Verticillium infection starting at the tip, moving up the pulp. Resembles a burnt cigar tip.

sap_burn.title: Sap Burn (Latex Burn)
sap_burn.summary: Black necrotic streaks caused by fruit sap.
sap_burn.detailed_analysis: Caustic sap (terpenes) burns the skin. Highly susceptible to secondary rot.
sap_burn.action: Sort and sell as second grade.

bacterial_canker.title: Bacterial Canker (Scab)
bacterial_canker.detailed_analysis: Xanthomonas infection. Causes raised, corky lesions that crack the skin.

lenticel_discoloration.title: Lenticel Spotting
lenticel_discoloration.summary: Darkened pores across the skin surface.
lenticel_discoloration.detailed_analysis: Stress response from cold or detergents. Skin pores turn dark, appearing like tiny freckles.

stem_end_rot.title: Stem-End Rot (SER)
stem_end_rot.summary: Internal decay starting at the stem.
stem_end_rot.detailed_analysis: Fungi enter during harvest and remain dormant until ripening. Rapidly turns pulp watery and brown.
stem_end_rot.action: Reject affected fruit; perform cut tests.

spongy_tissue.title: Internal Spongy Tissue
spongy_tissue.detailed_analysis: Internal metabolic disorder. Pulp becomes corky/spongy with a fermented odor. Invisible externally.

jelly_seed.title: Jelly Seed
jelly_seed.summary: Glassy, translucent pulp around the stone.
jelly_seed.detailed_analysis: Premature ripening of the pulp directly around the seed while outer flesh stays firm.

soft_nose.title: Soft Nose
soft_nose.summary: Premature softening at the fruit tip (apex).
soft_nose.detailed_analysis: Calcium deficiency related. The tip ripens days before the rest of the fruit.

thrips_ridging.title: Thrips Scarring
thrips_ridging.detailed_analysis: Surface feeding damage. Skin becomes thick and corky but pulp is perfect.
causes: High thrips population during fruit set

lenticel_damage.title: Lenticel Blackening
lenticel_damage.summary: Tiny black spots on green skin.
lenticel_damage.detailed_analysis: Damage to skin pores from abrasion or moisture. Can lead to moisture loss.

sunburn_corking.title: Sunburn (Corking)
sunburn_corking.summary: Yellow or brown hard, woody patches.
sunburn_corking.detailed_analysis: UV damage makes skin brittle and hard. Pulp underneath may become stringy.

mesocarp_browning.title: Internal Flesh Browning
mesocarp_browning.summary: Diffuse grey/brown patches in the pulp.
mesocarp_browning.detailed_analysis: Enzymatic browning of the edible flesh. Caused by cellular stress or old age.

vascular_browning.title: Vascular Browning (Fibre)
vascular_browning.detailed_analysis: Oxidation of the vascular bundles. Common in late-season fruit.

anthracnose_rot.title: Internal Anthracnose
anthracnose_rot.summary: Hemispherical rot moving into the seed.
anthracnose_rot.detailed_analysis: Skin spots that penetrate the pulp in a 'cone' shape. Flesh turns black and bitter.

seed_cavity_browning.title: Seed Cavity Browning
seed_cavity_browning.summary: Darkening of the space around the stone.
seed_cavity_browning.detailed_analysis: Common in fruit stored at very low but non-freezing temperatures.
